codeReview.summary: Re-review confirms urgency/pressure/drift functionality is present, but the previously reported blocking drift-classification bug is still present in current code.

issues.description: `detect_horizon_drift` still skips `(None -> Some(_))` as "not a shift" (line 844), then classifies with `shifts.iter().all(|s| *s > 0)` without guarding for an empty `shifts` vector. Because `all()` is true on empty iterators, first-time horizon assignment is incorrectly labeled `Postponement` instead of a baseline non-shift state (expected `Stable`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: H6 drift validation does not include a first-time horizon assignment case (`None -> Some`), which let the empty-iterator misclassification slip through.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md covers VAL-HDYN-010..015 for stable/postponement/repeated/tightening/loosening/oscillating, but no assertion for initial set; sd-core/src/dynamics.rs tests around lines 5708-5936 also omit a None->Some classification test.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker procedure requires `cargo doc -p sd-core --no-deps`, but this session still reports procedure compliance without that step.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Step 6 in .factory/skills/rust-library-worker/SKILL.md requires cargo doc; handoff for session 85d6c8b7-f121-4f26-8e9d-3ef6949a642e lists test/clippy/fmt only and sets `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Fail: the prior blocking concern remains unresolved. detect_horizon_drift still misclassifies first-time horizon assignment as Postponement due to an empty-iterator `all()` path.
